channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Why Pokemon cards are one of the hottest speculative booms right now
item.description: If you collected Pokemon cards as a kid, here's hoping you held onto them. The Japanese franchise has been popular for decades, but it's become the latest speculative boom.
item.content:encoded: If you collected Pokemon cards as a kid, here's hoping you held onto them. The Japanese franchise has been popular for decades, but it's become the latest speculative boom.
item.dc:creator: Ricky Mulvey

item.title: Actor Tim Blake Nelson feels like he's running out of time to do everything he wants
item.description: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Tim Blake Nelson talks about feeling like he's running out of time to do the things he wants.
item.content:encoded: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Tim Blake Nelson talks about feeling like he's running out of time to do the things he wants.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Martin

item.title: The most memorable moon movies aren't even about space
item.description: While marveling at Artemis II's mission, NPR's film critic went down a rabbit hole about moon-themed movies. Most have nothing to do with space.
item.content:encoded:
Paper Moon.'/>

While marveling at Artemis II's mission, NPR's film critic went down a rabbit hole about moon-themed movies. Most have nothing to do with space.

(Image credit: Cinematic)
item.dc:creator: Bob Mondello

item.title: What draws people into cults? A new book tracks the journeys of two followers
item.description: Harrison Hill's book The Oracle's Daughter is a story about the terror of losing the self — but it's also, gratifyingly, a story about finding the way back to it.
item.content:encoded: Harrison Hill's book The Oracle's Daughter is a story about the terror of losing the self — but it's also, gratifyingly, a story about finding the way back to it.
item.dc:creator: Ilana Masad

item.title: How a cheese sandwich at the Masters is still $1.50 in an era of price hikes
item.description: The priciest concessions at the Masters, beer and wine, cost just $6 each. The Georgia golf tournament prides itself on a simple and affordable menu, even as ticket prices continue to climb.
item.content:encoded:
The priciest concessions at the Masters, beer and wine, cost just $6 each. The Georgia golf tournament prides itself on a simple and affordable menu, even as ticket prices continue to climb.

(Image credit: Andrew Redington)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: This doctor turned a 31-foot RV into one of the country's only mobile OB-GYN clinics
item.description: Mary Fariba Afsari's book, Labor, is a portrait of reproductive healthcare in post-Dobbs America. Her book also is about her Iranian heritage and her grandmother's death from an illegal abortion.
item.content:encoded: Mary Fariba Afsari's book, Labor, is a portrait of reproductive healthcare in post-Dobbs America. Her book also is about her Iranian heritage and her grandmother's death from an illegal abortion.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: 'After the Flood' argues Bob Dylan's late career is just as potent as his early years
item.description: Much of our image of Dylan derives from his early protest music, but Robert Polito's book makes the argument that the most recent 30 years of Dylan's career have been just as creative as the first 30.
item.content:encoded: Much of our image of Dylan derives from his early protest music, but Robert Polito's book makes the argument that the most recent 30 years of Dylan's career have been just as creative as the first 30.
item.dc:creator: Ken Tucker

item.title: Hilariously caustic 'Big Mistakes' drags Dan Levy into organized crime
item.description: The new Netflix comedy created by Dan Levy and Rachel Sennott stars Levy as an uptight pastor and Taylor Ortega as his sister.
item.content:encoded:
The new Netflix comedy created by Dan Levy and Rachel Sennott stars Levy as an uptight pastor and Taylor Ortega as his sister.

(Image credit: Spencer Pazer)
item.dc:creator: Glen Weldon

item.title: 11 new books in April offer a chance to step inside someone else's world
item.description: The books we're spotlighting this month don't exactly radiate escapist good vibes — but they do offer the opportunity to step into someone else's life and get to know their view of our shared world.
item.content:encoded: The books we're spotlighting this month don't exactly radiate escapist good vibes — but they do offer the opportunity to step into someone else's life and get to know their view of our shared world.
item.dc:creator: Colin Dwyer

item.title: A humorist faces life with Stage 4 lung cancer: 'The future disappeared for me'
item.description: In 2020, Annabelle Gurwitch went to urgent care for a COVID-19 test and learned she had cancer. She writes about life as a "cancer slacker" in her memoir, The End of My Life is Killing Me.
item.content:encoded: In 2020, Annabelle Gurwitch went to urgent care for a COVID-19 test and learned she had cancer. She writes about life as a "cancer slacker" in her memoir, The End of My Life is Killing Me.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross
